race.name: Muskoka Loppet
race.tagline: Thirty kilometers of classic skiing through the frozen forests of Arrowhead Provincial Park — Ontario's signature Nordic race in Canada's cottage country.

vitals.registration: Online registration, capacity limited by park access
vitals.aid_stations: 2-3 stations with water, hot drinks, snacks

climate.description: The Muskoka region experiences classic Canadian winters with reliable snow cover. January temperatures typically range from -20°C to -5°C. Lake-effect moisture from Georgian Bay contributes to consistent snowfall. Cold, dry conditions dominate.
challenges: Extreme cold in January — wind chill can reach -30°C
Hard-packed icy tracks in extended cold snaps
Short January daylight limits warm-up time
Hilly terrain in frozen conditions demands strong descending skills

course.primary: Loops through Arrowhead Provincial Park on rolling Canadian Shield terrain. The course winds through mixed hardwood and conifer forest, with enough hills to test fitness without being overwhelming. Classic-only format emphasizes traditional technique on well-set tracks.
course.surface: Machine-groomed classic tracks through provincial park forest trails
course.grooming: Good grooming maintained by park staff and volunteers, classic tracks only
features: Classic-only format — pure traditional Nordic skiing
Beautiful Canadian Shield forest setting in Arrowhead Provincial Park
Muskoka cottage country setting — cozy post-race atmosphere

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: A well-established classic-only race with 40 years of history. The provincial park setting provides a controlled and scenic environment. Reliable Ontario winters ensure good snow. Modest field but loyal regional following.

history.summary: Founded in 1985, the Muskoka Loppet has been a fixture of the Ontario Nordic skiing community for four decades. Held in Arrowhead Provincial Park near Huntsville, the race takes advantage of the park's well-maintained trail system and the Muskoka region's reliable winter conditions. The classic-only format has remained a deliberate choice, preserving the traditional character of the event. The race draws participants from across Ontario and beyond, serving as one of the province's premier Nordic competitions.
notable_facts: Over 40 years of continuous operation in Arrowhead Provincial Park
Classic-only format preserves traditional Nordic skiing ethos
Arrowhead Provincial Park is known for its natural ice skating trail and winter recreation
The Muskoka region is one of Ontario's most popular four-season recreation areas
The race has been a qualifier and stepping stone for Canadian long-distance skiers
